title: Fried Crispy Duck Biryani
description: A rich, crunchy monsoon Chinese dish featuring orange zest in a halal-friendly recipe.
instructions: Preheat oil in a deep-fryer to 375°F (190°C).
Season duck with salt and pepper. Fry the skin-side down until golden brown, about 8-10 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before flipping to fry the other side. Brown well and set aside.
Drain off excess oil from the frying pan used for the duck, then add a bit more oil if needed. Add ginger paste and orange zest, sauté until fragrant.
Add rice and stir-fry it in the mixture of oil and spices to ensure the grains are lightly coated with the flavors.
Return the fried duck pieces to the pan. Pour soy sauce over everything and mix well, allowing flavors to blend thoroughly. Cook for about 5 minutes more.
Pour the crispy duck pieces on top of the rice mixture in the bottom of a large, oven-proof dish. Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake at 320°F (160°C) for approximately 40-50 minutes or until golden brown and crispy on all sides.
